column35: Comments

First game I was getting recruit locked. By the time I realized it, it was even difficult to get an officer. I did not make that mistake again. It was nice to get Sharon Carter. (She helped me get Nomor, the Sub-Mariner’s rare early on and then went undercover to not clog my deck.) Yo-Yo helped with Immortus. The second game was really all luck and very little skill, that was no fun. First game was a lot more fun but 33 turns was not going to cut it in this tournament.

Karnak and his When Recruited powers were key to manage the city early in the game. Hand 2 built up with Captain Marvel and Lady Thor and shined later in the game. Heralds of Galactus were not super useful, except for Silver Surfer which is a great card.
Was hoping to eat whole city, but only got to Bridge. Heralds are monsters if properly supplied with cold hard cash, and I added two richest girls around :D

Getting 4 master strikes in the first 10 turns was a rough start, but one hand eventually got online with turbo KO's and massive damage while the other generated all the recruit in the world. Did the funny with galactus, but figured the extra turns and possible more escapes probably isn't worth the VP or risk of Cthon. In the end, the only survivor was the sewers.

Both our games went decently well other than them both taking way longer than we hoped. Ended up having to choose a higher points per turn or less total turns. Medusa and werewolf worked great with us pretty much just ignoring the heralds.

There was a lot going on here! I managed to keep Chthon away and felt mostly in control. Even focused for Galactus a few times in my first game but realized Logan+Karnak plus Surfer for some draw support was a more reliable combo (hit ~40 attack on my last turn). The focus deck (Psi Lord, Proxima, Heralds) had great synergy for support but got really big and maybe slowed me down. Ended up recruiting fast to reveal some more Karnak cards for the other side to end it. Logan's Loner was the real hero without other rares showing!
Galactus sure did hunger

Crazy tough setup! My “Hail Mary” for defeating Chthon was Namor’s rare but it didn’t show up. Although, I did summon Galactus, but it was far too late. I think these hero choices could work, but it really depends on how things shuffle. In my case, both the hero deck and villain deck shuffles were just bad.
This was one of the most fun tournament setups I've played. The enemy rush mechanics kept me on edge both games!

Games were opposite. First game was everything but Mandroids. They were 9 attack by the time they showed up. Second game was great. Both decks had piercing energy with Legion's Split Personality/Split Eardrums. But even better the Mandroids kept showing up and getting cycled back in allowing a lot of KO. Still couldn't avoid an escape with the masterstrike, Thor Corp Agent Marshall.

column31: I did not play a second attempt.
column32: I did not play a second attempt.

column35: Another melk-curdling round

column31: I did not play a second attempt.
column32: I did not play a second attempt.
column35: Taskmaster's Thunderbolts were a mean addition! Thankfully we only saw 2 Twists so the Mandroids were manageable and provided some much needed KO. Stature went a bit wild in my deck, stomping on the Shield Elite to give me big bonuses, which I eventually used to take out Maria. My wife started off fighting the big stuff, but then had to resort to clearing the City for me to hit the Mastermind. Not a 'fun' set up, but a decent challenge and an interesting puzzle.

This looked to be a very hard setup, but then I remembered we could 'cheat' by using Piercing Energy. So I added Polaris and Banshee and honestly, both games felt like a breeze, at least to tackle the Scheme part of the puzzle. WIth Maria Hill, especially in the beginning, the city is going to flood and this was a huge problem in both my attempts. However, in game 1 I couldn't prevent 1 late game escape (of Jester). In my second attempt I aimed at doing better in the escape part, but completely failed, partly due to a worse start from the Villain Deck (Master Strike very early). So I couldn't get that dream online, it even became 2 escapes pretty soon. After that I could manage it OK, but eventually a third escape happened. I was like: "aw, shit, this might have been a waste of time.". but, it was a game with exactly as many VP as the first but 9 turns faster. So I gambled a bit and went ahead to submit the second attempt.

Decbuilding Wise, I just built the Banshee cards, together with some Polaris and Phoenix for Piercing Energy. It actually bit me in the ass a bit as well, as sometimes you'd just get one PE too few and it was like a hand full of wounds (due to Thor Corps and S.H.I.E.L.D. Officers requiring actual Attack points). This sorted itself out in a way though. In game 1 Deck 2 got Polaris' rare very earrly on and it made everything that much simpler. In Attempt 2 however, I got Banshee's rare into deck 1, while deck2 had the brunt of the other Piercing Energy cards. So both decks could hit Maria sometimes without needing to clear the annoying S.H.I.E.L.D. Officers in the city. I guess Piercing Energy isn't OP, but it sure made things a lot easier. Especially the Mandroid Super Sentinels. Those were just insanely strong very early on in both games. (Game 2 I drew 3 Scheme Twists in a row). It however, didn't matter at all as 1 PE could defeat 1 Mandroid. Game 1 had a great start with this, getting 3 mandroids with 1 Phoenix 4-cost. Game 2 didn't get as lucky, but the strategy was sound and luck/execution was while not objectively 'better' (counting the 2 extra escapes as a big loss), at least a lot 'faster'.

I think I played on the higher side of average this game, but I'm not sure if everyone picks the same heroes as I did. If they do, others might have an even better execution/luck strike and get it done even faster and/or with fewer escapes and/or more points. Game 1 felt like a drag, I think I drew many more Mandroids in total, as the Villain Deck at the end was the same size as after attempt 2, but it lasted 9 turns longer. Getting lucky with the flips from the Villain Deck is a huge one in this setup too, as more points is better, but getting the required attack/PE for those bigger cards is not a guarantee and might actually risk more escapes.

KO-ing was very good in both games, getting a few early KO's in due to mandroids and some from Polaris' 6-costs and Phoenix' 3 costs. The Thor Corps and S.H.I.E.L.D. Officers felt almost as bad to gain as wounds though, although they surely helped build the more expensive cards. Soaring Flight is one of the greatest/strongest keywords in my opinion, because it can guarantee getting a 7-cost in turn 3/4 (from Polaris' recruit card) or 2/4 PE for either Banshee's 3-cost/Phoenix' 4-cost) the immediate next turn (for the same deck). It was awesome to play this. I don't give it a 5/5, because I think it was kind of mean, but letting us pick PE heroes wasn't mean :). Also, it didn't feel that fun, I think because of Thor Corps and Taskmaster's Thunderbolts. You might know me well enough by now to understand I think more points equals more fun (which might not always be true, but I'd say it's an overall trend which doesn't break easily). I don't think there were that many points to be had in this setup, so that's another reason for a 4/5 and not a 5/5. I did have a blast though. Let's find out what's in store for next week!

I just don't like Maria Hill. She is so frustrating to play against. My first game got close to an easy win around turn 20 when my decks started to get some synergy, but then the inevitable clog of Officers began and it was the usual struggle to make a decent hand after that. I felt proud of only 3 escapes, but it was a slog.

column31: I did not play a second attempt.
column32: I did not play a second attempt.
column35: It was rough with Hill’s double shield clearance. I was reluctant to KO starters for that very reason so I had rather bloated decks but with piercing energy around it helped. The hard part was still having enough fight to get rid of shield officer villains in the city as I couldn’t use piecing energy on them. Argh. Led to some dead turns and this was the closest ever. The last card was a acheme twist and that would have made a draw!

column31: I did not play a second attempt.
column32: I did not play a second attempt.
column35: Instinct side took off, eventually getting cap's rare and replaying it with double agent. Wolvie tucked a twist for extra safety

This was a lot of fun. Had to keep in mind that there was little recruitment in the hero deck. That is were the first attempt faltered. The second attempt was way better. Venompools uncommon was raking those bystanders in. Other hand was a Man out of Time deck. It was so much fun! MVP was definitively Venompool!

Lost the first game due to a bad shuffle. 6 twists at turn 24. I decided to play 2 more cards from Cap 75th because I figured I wouldn’t have an opportunity to play them again in the tournament. 3 caps played very nicely together but it was “unbeatable squirrel girl” that delivered the final blow which switcherooed in Steve Roger’s “save the world”. Best game I’ve played in quite a while.
Extra cheese venompooled into digesting bystanders. Pizza Steve rogered into rescuing bystanders until he had 8 cards in his deck. He was buying shield officers just to ko them for bystanders.

We had good results with Jessica and Carnage in our first match, so we just ran it back with the same duo to see if we could improve our results. In the second match we had better luck with Scheme Twists and getting the cards we wanted in the HQ, and we both got Savior earlier. Jessica is one of my favorite characters so it was nice to bring her along, and Carnage was triggering his Extreme Violence often for us. Fun setup!

Game 1 was decent if a little slow. I managed to Switcheroo into 2 copies of Can I Get a Little Gratitude? Which was great giving extra rescue effects and extra Bystanders. Eg I rescued the Cloning Technician and then got to buy three copies of Hungry for Action for the price of one. Never saw Play to the Crowd which would have helped pick up the pace. Game 2 did say Play to the Crowd, which worked well, but while I finished earlier, overall that game didn’t go as well, I let a Villain escape early on and I didn’t collect nearly as many extra Bystanders/VP, so I decided to submit game 1.

column31: I did not play a second attempt.
column32: I did not play a second attempt.
column35: Fun set up! Really had to LEAN into heroes that could rescue bystanders! Venompool and Howard the Duck helped in this regard.

Really solid gameplay, I felt pretty confident about the heroes I selected. I think Venompools Shenanigans card is very strong as long as you play around it. I actually felt a lot better about my second attempt but looking at the numbers my first attempt just barely outdid my second attempt. Great game

Nightcrawler/Silver Surfer didn't work out the first game, so I switched to Wolverine/Scarlet Spider. Might not have been their fault as I drew 7 twists in the first 20 turns! Logan and Kaine pulled their weight and then some. Had a couple turns of Kaine's rare coming up and getting to draft some cheap Wall Crawl heroes to immediately play. Even brought two of Terrax down to 0 Attack on the same turn. The other hand was drawing constantly with Wolverine dropping 6+ attack almost every turn from just a couple cards. Very happy with how this turned out and was really able to get behind the theme!

column31: I did not play a second attempt.
column32: I did not play a second attempt.
column35: The reverse BagelShuffle helped a lot as the top 21 cards contained only 1 scheme twist. X-Force Wolvie, Symbiote Spidey, and Elektra carried the team for AntDude with WaspWoman struggling to get traction with Mockingbird and Iron Fist. never saw Torch. A nice setup, but Spider decks do seem like a little too much cheese for a really enjoyable game sometimes.

we had a great time playing with the heroes! I had a pretty sick colossus human torch wounds deck, managed to get colossus rare, and my friend had an insane elektra symbiote deck sprinkled with mockingjay and bishop 2 cost. He got both electra and symbiote uncommon in one turn which was nuts!

I went in thinking that PC and NC will both help in: a) having red card in hand, b) having more than 6 cards in hand when Sinister strikes. Also did the math for the scheme (that is if after first 4 twists you only KO two non-grey heroes, you are good until twist 8). It worked quite well in first game, with Mockingbird also producing lots of extra VP. Also Morg Villain helped keeping the HQ yellow/red mostly. Still there were some hickups, could have been bit faster in building up enough damage. Second game was bit faster (27 turns), but only 3 VP/turn average. It's not trivial choice. In both games, my second hand (SSpiderman+Elektra) had less time to shine, due to slower buildup. Second game Coulson, Mockingbird and Maggia Goons helped in getting rid of all 12 starting cards by the last turn (basically losing one card each turn!). So it was fun and interesting.

There were so many options with hero's on this setup, it's almost impossible to decide if I made the correct choice. Electro and symbiote spider both lend themselves to a low cost deck. Mockingbird suggests shield level and access to the fantastic four is always beneficial. In the end decided that invisible woman and Mr Fantastic were just too good to resist.
Great combination of characters.  Mastermind was enough of a challenge.

The first attempt had a better showing for Jean and her gross bystander engine. The second attempt got wrecked by twists 5-7 and KOed 3 rares. Luckily, the elektra-symbiote deck carried early and often for the win. Only KOed 17/18 non grey
The non-spider deck initially had a bad time, constantly needing to get recruiters and wasting stars while doing so. This eventually led to getting ultimate Elektra, Mockingbird, & Human Torch.
Symbiote Spider-Man and Elektra was a deadly combo that hit the mastermind for the first time on like turn 6. Sadly Mockingbird took way too long to get moving.
Cool hydra hunting my heros'  2 pointer recuit🤣

Brought Star Lord T'Challa to help activate Crystal's ranged common. Brought Blade, Daywalker primarily for his killer uncommon, since it provides both early KO, and knowledge for What If...?. Ended up picking up 3 copies of that uncommon very early(2 were in the starting HQ)!! Snash108 ended up with no deck or discard  and 20 attack. Hank even tried to mess him with a wound, but Captain Carter blocked it, and provided even more attack!! Couldn't have asked for better luck!!

Lost attempt 1 on turn 8, with 4 wagered souls in 8 turns. Won attempt 2 in 17 turns, winning every single wager. My two Hero picks of Blade and WWBN were the MVPs, shredding through one of my decks with Hunt High and Low and Snarling Fangs. I generated 16 attack on the last turn, had no 0-costs in my deck, an hit Pym 4 times in a row. Very legendary Legendary moment.

I'm a gambling man so I love blackjack. I just wish if I get a 21, the enemy Mastermind doesn't get a 21 as well. Ugh. However, I was able to get an early Thor rare which allowed me to basically get the rares onto one side and gather huge strength turns with the removal of my gray cards early on.

With this, I was basically able to cycle through my cards almost every turn to get the win. I just want to win a game of blackjack! JUST ONE!

I love the Scheme though, its everything I want in a unique scheme that plays itself out.

Thor Rare plus Elsa rare in one turn caught me 24-26 attack in one turn. Much love.

I ended up not really getting any Werewolf by Night cards, but Apocalyptic Black Widow's liberate was very helpful to deal with Pym. Party Thor and Clea helped thin the deck, too. Somehow I won every game of blackjack in game one...that was not the case in game two. I thought the villains, mastermind, and scheme played well together and made it sting lest when I discarded all the good heroes I recruited to at least hit Pym.
We were on the brink of busting for a 4th time each round, which made it very exciting! Hero synergy was very interesting and Hank pym is super fun

Never played this scheme before and I thought it was going to be bad, but it was actually kind of fun, despite being pretty much all luck.
My plan to KO all my starters failed both games, so instead I stuffed my decks with cards to water down the 0 costs and tried to get lots of card draw.
Getting a copy of The Shield of Britain in both games emboldened me to go after Pym even if I wasn't sure I had enough \\\

I really enjoy playing against Hank Pym, and the hero choices and the opportunity to play around with Demonic Bargain in the midst of Pym's antics created a lot of really cool dynamics. There's a bunch about this setup that really works and I loved! But It never feels good to know that the scheme is gonna do what the scheme is gonna do no matter what; that there's no real skill involved with simply surviving long enough to defeat the mastermind. Game 2 only took 15 turns to lose -_- 

With that said, Party Thor ended up working much better than I expected him too, and I was REALLY happy with my hero picks. When luck was on my side and I actually got to play, the decks I was able to build felt really good.

Edited doesn't like this scheme, but I love it. We got wrecked by it both times though...On our second attempt I lost with a 21 and 20 for the first two twists... this week along with our first week placement will be the reason we don't make the cut. Oh well, can't say it hasn't been fun so far! By normal practices I wouldn't get to participate next week so I am happy with the new format.
This is my second and legal submission after the illegal mistakes I made in my first game

This Scheme is awful. It is the opposite of fun. I hated it so much that by the end of game 2 I was tempted to put my entire Legendary collection on eBay. It literally turns the game into Candyland - whether you've won or lost is pretty much determined after you finish shuffling the decks, you've just got to waste an hour of your life finding out which it is.

Hank is an interesting Mastermind and I was starting to get my brain around how to work with the sloppy mess of 7 Heroes. But after 8 straight blackjack losses and a grand total of about 25 turns across two games, I truly couldn't care less. And I quite seriously think I wouldn't have cared even if I'd been reliably winning at blackjack, because there's zero skill involved. You may as well just roll a D6 and lose on 4 or less.

Please, I beg of you, never ever use this scheme in a tournament again.

column2: The Deck is Grey and Full of Terrors
column35: Black Widow rocks!! 2 copies of her uncommon showed up in the starting HQ, and because of the scheme, I had reliable bystander income to fuel them! Even though her commons never really showed, I still smashed Magneto's face in. Other side scored  Bishop's rare early, but didn't get rolling quite as fast!

column31: I did not play a second attempt.
column32: I did not play a second attempt.
column35: Professor X pulling in Jean Grey Villains as grey Heroes. Good times.
First game started really rough. 3 scheme twists and 2 Jean Grey cards in the first 6. That didn't end well. All stars of the second game were Prof X's mind control and Black Widow.

I wish that Magneto was harder. He deserves to be harder. He’s one of Marvel’s most iconic villains. Using Forge and Domino as my choices was almost unfair combined with the static choices, as I was going for pure speed and power since I knew Magneto would be an easy takedown. I feel like I succeeded in that endeavor, along with some lucky draws. The KO in this setup really helped a lot as well.
Nil

First round, Black Widow did what I thought she'd do.  Powering up Widow almost didn't seem fair.  There was no  question about winning, but just how quick.  2nd round - no Widow so had to play for reals.  Got taken to the very edge but battled back for a win, just not as good as the first round.  Very fun setup.  Dig the scheme.  Magneto needs a buff and an epic side put put him among the MasterMind greats.
I won both games, difficult to choose which one was better, because we used two different strategies. Good scheme and mastermind combination for a speed game.

Had more escapes than I wanted and quite a few more turns too. Managed to get 2 of Punishers "defeat the top card of the VD" and managed to farm some extra VP with that. Pretty fun overall, though my hero choices were unconventional and likely going to cost me in the end.

column31: I did not play a second attempt.
column32: I did not play a second attempt.
column35:
I was hoping that forge alone could withstand the phalanx. He did fine enough. I had a shot early at diving block with six recruit turn one but a Magneto had different plans and struck me masterfully. That would’ve gone a long way with in a tech deck but it wasn’t meant to be. 
Right hand did end up getting BFG but never got a shot off. 
Left wanted to be range based with infusion of covert to instigate some KO. Did alright. It never popped off really well, just a little bit. 
Some considerate late master strike allowed me to string together four consecutive turns punching magneto in the face, securing the victory. 
In the end, though, Magneto was wrong. And the professor is right.

First attempt we had several scheme twists and Jean Grey's off the top of the villain deck, giving us tons of demon goblins and goblin queens to fight in the early game. Second game we had a better shuffle and a better score as a result. Really just came down to the shuffle.
First attempt went so well. Second attempt made me remember that Apocalypse is not very nice.

column31: I did not play a second attempt.
column32: I did not play a second attempt.
column35: I found Apocalypse stressful and frustrating at times since I essentially had to skip two turns (one per hand) every time his mastermind tactic came out. I thought the mechanic of empowering his Horsemen was thematic and not too difficult. I had to try very hard to balance the scheme to make sure I didn't let 6 non grey heroes get KOed so that meant I avoided fighting Death. I also got lucky that the top of the deck discards for the scheme were almost always a starter card. Rictor was very, very handy since his rare lets you shatter the mastermind. I was able to use this card a few times on Apocalypse and it probably saved the game for me. I didn't like the clone mechanic for Predator X since it almost caused an escape a couple of times.

column31: I did not play a second attempt.
column32: I did not play a second attempt.
column35: One of the most daunting setups I have ever played.  It requires incredible luck, skill, and timing to beat.  I don't know how, but I pulled it off.  One of my most satisfying wins of all time

No tech or strength to fight off the Phalanx and scheme twists was brutal. Not to mention soooooo many villains have a chance to KO a non-grey hero. That plus Apocalypse nerfing your hand is an awful combo. Managed to hit Apocalypse twice in my second game, but the first one was resurrected and the second lost me the game with KOs. Cannot reccomend anyone try a setup based on 6 non-grey KOs with this many ways to KO those heroes. In my first game I tried to bring the tech and strength myself, but it wasn't good enough. In my second game I went all in on ranged, covert, and instinct, and it still wasn't enough. I think my second game had a potentially winning strategy, you just need to get some strength sidekicks early to stave off some twists.  Seriously unfun, sorry RedBlock. A+ for theme though!

Wasn't a fan of Karma. Rictor was fun to play with. The Scheme Killed us easy the 1st attempt. We would have been able to fight the Master Mind more if the Master Strike didn't make us get rid of cards so easy haha. We enjoyed playing though. Much better than last game.

I am sorry, but this setup was just stupidly challenging :D I really don't mind losing if it is an interesting and entertaining game, but this was frustrating, constantly worrying about the KOs, or not being able to do anything because of the master strikes. My first game ended after 11 turns. My second game was a lot better, I think I was really lucky with some of the shuffles, but it wasn't enough to win. I might give this game a couple more tries just to see if it is winnable or not without being extremely lucky. Also I am curious what heroes did the others use. I chose Iron Fist for his strength cards an Punisher for his tech cards to help with the scheme and the henchmen.
took a rick not including green in the hero deck, trying to focus on hero synergy. and was doing well the second game till death escaped, we went form 0 non grey kos, to 6 in like 3 turns
This one proved too much of a challenge for me.
No words, just bad.

column2: One Skrull, Two Skrull, Red Skull, Blue Skull
column35:
The core set is so strong. I had two bad starts but pulled it out thanks to drawing Thor's ult and killing Red Skull in one shot, ha.

I would have liked a little more variety. This is the second week I have been stuck with mostly just the core set. That has nothing to do with the setup though. Also, I am learning that 3 attempts would feel much better than 2.

We actually learnt to appreciate Spiderman a bit more than normal. We wish Upperdeck would reprint the core deck with different art on all of the cards. It was good for a core set up though, we do love a lot of the other add ins though (especially missed all the great bystanders and sidekicks etc.)

It was kinda fun to play just Core again, but reminds me why the original Core game gets a little boring.  No fun bystanders, sidekicks, etc.  That said, it was fun for what it was.  My first attempt, I tried Iron-Man and Hawkeye. Deck 1 focused on Tech and Instinct card draw, Deck 2 did Range and Strength.  This worked out fairly well though once again, Deck 1 did all the heavy lifting while Deck 2 struggled.  Game was shorter at 23 total turns, and Player 1 generated 70 total attack in that turn to easily end the game.  Attempt 2 subbed Captain America in for Hawkeye with hopes of leveraging the multi-color decks.  Iron Man card draw stayed away from the HQ however, and it took a while to get moving.  It didn't help that Master Strikes only popped twice, and Endless Armies combined with lots of skrulls in the city early.  The high attack requirements and threats led to more escapes and a lower scoring game that took longer.

column31: I did not play a second attempt.
column32: I did not play a second attempt.
column35: Xmen heroes work so well together

column31: I did not play a second attempt.
column32: I did not play a second attempt.
column35: I keep on learning from this event. I tried once and it taught me to make sure that I had all of the correct cards in each deck. I got almost to the end and I found out then. I will not make that mistake again. I am not counting that as an attempt. (If that's incorrect please feel free to give me all zeros this week. lol) I enjoyed getting to play and have fun. I really like how this scheme allowed the heroes to become the bad guys!! Skrulls!! who knew?!?!? lol

I tried to extend my turns in the second game in order to get more victory points but I think the scheme coupled with the heroes I chose didn't really give me a massive advantage in collecting a high number for victory points. A fun game for sure. I hope I get some expansions next week. I'm running out of good characters from the core set haha.

both games felt really held back and vanilla, just pôwering through the turns, either the decks or hq got cluttered fast and i was thinking about swapping capt. america for someone with k.o. power but there weren't rly any choices for it imo cuz both black widow and rogue need 'red' for it to trigger. both games took exactly 29 turns and i submitted the one with 2 less escapes which had 4vp and 6bystanders less.
we'll see how it goes it feels very middle or low end of the pack, but i could also not be the only one that feels like this.

Really fun games. I faired slightly better on my second attempt although struggled with getting decent attack as most cards in the HQ were recruit. 2 villains escaped early on in the game but managed to keep the city under control for the most part.

column31: I did not play a second attempt.
column32: I did not play a second attempt.
column35: This one revealed that the base game still holds up as a fun game ti play on its own. Yes the later expansions add so much variety but the core is still good.

column31: I did not play a second attempt.
column32: I did not play a second attempt.
column35: Complicated, but fun.

Both were super fast games for us (which is probably scary that others were even faster). I love S.H.I.E.L.D. - don't get that expansion out that much but enjoyed running one side Phil + Death-lock sending all initial cards undercover for points.

I ended up with 2 very different games.

Game 1 I couldn't get Psi-Lord to catch any Bystanders or Strikes, and ended up with a slim Surfer/Brainstorm deck that took down the mastermind in a few back-to-back turns (thanks to Kang's tactic and Brainstorm's rare). I was able to get a few Villains off the top of the Villain Deck with Psi-Lord's uncommon too. This game went somewhat quickly with 24 turns, but I drew mostly Strikes and Twists, so I didn't rack up that many points.

Game 2 had a slow start. I think I had 9 twists in the first 20 turns of the game, losing to the mastermind in 5 of the contests. I held on, but would have ended the game around turn 28 with about the same PPT as Game 1. So I kept playing. Ended up with nearly a full Deathlock deck for one player and a Surfer deck again for the other. Both had a few Psi-Lord cards to collect bystanders and fight the top Villain Deck card. The game ended with all the bystanders rescued, 18 Shield heroes undercover, and the Villain Deck completely empty.

While Game 2 was definitely more fun, at 36 turns I think it would net me dead last in the turn scores. With 139 points it was still very tempting to turn that game in though.

Very fun games. Went with the same heroes on each setup, but totally different games. Both wins. The first was pretty methodical. I was hoping to use the Coulson/ Mockingbird/ Deathlok combo, but it didn't break that way and instead spammed Captain Mar-Vel and Silver Surfer. I got their rares pretty early and was able to use Danger Sense 7 several times to push as many scheme twists back as possible. Second game was way more swingy, had an escape, but got the engine I wanted. A lot more VP, but I was worried about the turns and the escape, so I went with Game #1.

I liked the scheme and Mastermind combo. I did not find either incredibly difficult, they did not hamper each other making either extraordinarily difficult. While one player found a great combo it was very one-sided with him popping off and the other plater just not.
Focus and SHIELD Level were a lot of fun!

column31: I did not play a second attempt.
column32: I did not play a second attempt.
column35:
We focused on a tech deck and a ranged deck. Deathlok paired with Mr. Fantastic sounded better then it worked out as the deck got working too well for Deathlok to send much undercover. It also had the advantage of Brainstorm’s Time Loop Experiment triggering One Giant hand.
Nova and Captain Mar-Vell with some support from Brainstorm borrowing from the future to trigger gaining shards and lots of card draw.
Lots of cool synergies crossing between heroes.

Accidentally made it harder on myself playing against EPIC Kang.  But was pretty luck with my left hand having the Rare Mr. Fantastic and Rare Brainstorm combo.  Brainstorm came out early and I was able to use her 7 total times in the game.

This was the first time I was able to really get the hang of Contest of Champions, this was one of those keywords that always gave me a hard time. I really enjoyed the mechanics and the thematic presentation.
Tough game.  Kept losing the Contest of Chanpmions

column35: Great Setup!!!

Slow start with several escapes (Henchmen were slow to show up), but once the decks came together they ROLLED! Both leaned into Bystanders, one more so with Hiroim and the other with Jean Grey. They overlapped with some cards, and Star-Lord, Spider-Woman, and Bishop served to round both out. Biship more so with Jean to maximize X-Men bonuses. I don't think I've had a game before this one where I recruited and played ALL of the rare cards! Really satisfying for them to all come together cohesively. Both decks got to the point of being fully trimmed (no starting cards) and drawing plus playing their entire contents each turn! My final Jean Grey turn developed 60+ attack and 20+ recruit to wallop an 18-fight Thanos twice to close out the win! The Reality Gem made it really easy to manage Thanos' Master Strikes, and then it became easy to keep the city clear, so protecting Baby Hope became trivial. I transitioned to the end game efficiently without wasted turns (no villains to fight in the city but not enough fight for Thanos). Really fun setup!

column35: Talk about coming down to the wire! Took Thanos out on the very last turn! Left hand was a Daredevil/Spiderwoman deck that tried to go for the deck manipulation strat. It mostly worked to get multiple rares (using SW’s rare to put DD’s rare on top of my deck was great!) and a few big turns at the end to hit the depowered Thanos. Right side tried to go a Spidey 2 cost deck, but ended up pivoting to Hiroim Bystander and got the final blow on Thanos with his transformed “Hiroim Redeemed.” Fun game!

Holy Dooley! A Super tough match...not sure if I just picked the wrong heroes, but what I feared and tried to mitigate, happened...and more! The 'Bound Souls' pile was terrible...crippled my turns to do any decent attack; most of the villains were at 7ATK or higher and I struggled racking up good ATK. This then cascaded to not being able to save Hope before a scheme twist whisked her away...luckily I had built enough ATK later on to actually save Hope from a villain...relieving the pressure.Thanos was under absolutely no pressure and never got touched...but having said that I'm glad a drew out a long loss!
